preamble: Consonantalization and fortition of the high back vowel [u] or its semivowel reflex [w] into the voiced velar stop [g] (often via an intermediate [gw]) is a classic example of strengthening, notably occurring when Romance languages adapted Germanic loanwords.

examples.note: Germanic [w] (vocalic reflex [u]) was adapted as [ɡw] and eventually [ɡ] in French, Spanish, and Italian.
Adaptation of the Germanic labial glide as a velar stop.
Loanword adaptation where initial [u/w] is reinforced with a velar onset [ɡ].
